key_insight: The paper models infrastructure inspection as a multi-stage stochastic optimization problem with endogenous uncertainty, and compares exacting optimization with reinforcement learning for scalable routing and scheduling.
engineering_relevance: It helps engineers plan limited inspection resources more reliably by choosing between high-quality optimization methods and faster learning-based policies for large infrastructure networks.
method: Formulate inspection routing/scheduling as a multi-stage stochastic mixed-integer program with failure dynamics.
Solve with stochastic dual dynamic integer programming (SDDiP) using dual approximation, integer state reduction, and sampling-based forward simulation.
Train a reinforcement learning policy to learn job clustering and routing decisions from simulated system behavior.
evidence_signal: Numerical experiments on infrastructure networks with different topologies and failure patterns show SDDiP gives high-quality solutions but scales poorly, while the learning approach scales better with competitive performance.
watch_item: The learning-based method is more scalable, but its performance depends on the quality of simulation and may trade off against the stronger solution guarantees of optimization.
paper_title: Multi-Stage Stochastic Optimization and Reinforcement Learning Approaches for Dynamic Inspection of Infrastructure Systems
